title: Module Manager Plan
goal: Create a centralized system to export, store, and manage TS/React modules so they can be reused across different projects without manual copying.

core_concepts.problem: AI Studio applications run in isolated containers. Code written in this app does not automatically transfer to new apps. The module manager must serve as an external bridge.

solutions.name: Supabase Remote Registry
solutions.rationale: Since the app already integrates db_supa.ts, modules and their metadata can be pushed directly to a Supabase database. New projects can dynamically fetch these scripts via Supabase API.

solutions.name: GitHub Sync (Recommended)
solutions.rationale: Pushing modules to a centralized GitHub repository allows new projects to fetch the code using raw URLs. This integrates well with existing AI tools (like read_url_content) to natively read in modules.

solutions.name: NPM Package Workflow
solutions.rationale: The module manager could bundle selected scripts and provide commands to publish them to NPM (e.g., @ziky_inc/modules). New apps would just 'npm install' the package.

implementation_steps: Step 1: Choose the storage medium (e.g., GitHub or Supabase).
Step 2: Build an 'Export Engine' in the current app that grabs the raw code of selected modules (using fetch on the file path) and pushes it to the storage.
Step 3: Define a standard 'Importer Prompt/Snippet' that you can paste into any **new** AI Studio environment to instruct the AI to pull and recreate the needed modules from the central registry.
Step 4: Gradually migrate existing modules out of this app and clear them locally, relying entirely on the manager's remote registry.
